screens.title: Fever & Infection Symptoms
screens.question: Do you currently have any of these common fever symptoms?

screens.title: Respiratory Symptoms
screens.question: Are you experiencing any respiratory issues?

screens.title: Digestive Symptoms
screens.question: Are you having any digestive or stomach issues?

screens.title: Food & Lifestyle Exposure
screens.question: Please answer about your recent eating habits and activities.

columns.text: Ate outside food recently (restaurant / street food / takeaway)
Consumed cold foods or drinks (ice cream, cold drinks)
Intense workout or heavy physical exertion recently
Travelled to a new place or different climate

screens.title: PCOS / Hormonal Symptoms
screens.question: Do you experience any of these menstrual or hormonal symptoms?

columns.text: Irregular periods (cycle longer than 35 days or unpredictable)
Mood swings, anxiety, or fatigue around periods
Excess facial or body hair (chin, upper lip, chest, abdomen)

screens.title: PCOS — Skin & Weight Symptoms
screens.question: Have you noticed any of the following skin or body changes?

columns.text: Dark skin patches on neck, underarms, or groin

screens.title: Obesity & Diet Analysis
screens.question: How would you describe your regular diet? (Select all that apply)

columns.text: I frequently consume sugary foods or sweets
I consume non-vegetarian food (meat, fish, eggs)
I skip meals or eat irregularly

screens.title: Physical Activity Level
screens.question: What best describes your activity and lifestyle?

columns.text: High intensity workouts 5+ times per week

items.title: For Fever & Infection
description: Rest adequately and stay hydrated — drink at least 8–10 glasses of water daily
Use a cool, damp cloth on your forehead to reduce fever discomfort
Avoid cold foods and drinks until symptoms fully resolve
Monitor temperature regularly and consult a doctor if fever exceeds 103°F

items.title: For Cold & Respiratory Issues
description: Steam inhalation with eucalyptus oil helps clear nasal congestion
Gargle with warm saltwater twice daily for sore throat
Avoid exposure to cold drafts and keep warm
Honey and ginger tea provides natural relief for cough and throat pain

items.title: For Nausea & Digestive Issues
description: Eat light, easily digestible foods like khichdi, boiled rice, or plain toast
Avoid oily, spicy, and heavy foods until digestive symptoms subside
Sip on ORS (Oral Rehydration Solution) to prevent dehydration
Ginger tea or warm lemon water can ease nausea effectively

items.title: Diet Adjustments for PCOS
description: Reduce refined carbohydrates: white rice, white bread, maida-based foods
Include more fibre-rich foods: vegetables, legumes, whole grains
Anti-inflammatory foods such as turmeric, green leafy vegetables, and berries help
Avoid sugary drinks and packaged juices — choose fresh fruit instead

items.title: Exercise & Lifestyle for PCOS
description: 30 minutes of moderate exercise daily (brisk walking, yoga, cycling) helps regulate hormones
Yoga poses like Supta Baddha Konasana and Bhujangasana support hormonal balance
Maintain a consistent sleep schedule — aim for 7–8 hours per night
Reduce stress through meditation, breathing exercises, or journaling

items.title: Healthy Eating Habits
description: Replace fried snacks with roasted nuts, seeds, fruits, or yoghurt
Eat smaller meals 4–5 times a day rather than 2–3 large meals
Use healthier cooking methods: steaming, baking, grilling instead of frying
Reduce sugar: limit sweets, mithai, biscuits and packaged snacks

items.title: Weight Loss Lifestyle Tips
description: Drink a large glass of water before each meal to reduce overeating
Avoid eating 2 hours before sleeping for better digestion and metabolism
Track your meals using a simple food diary or app to build awareness
Set realistic goals: 0.5–1 kg per week is healthy, sustainable weight loss

items.title: Consult a Doctor If You Have
description: Fever above 103°F lasting more than 3 days without improvement
Difficulty breathing, chest pain, or persistent severe cough
Periods absent for more than 3 consecutive months
Unusual or rapid weight gain without dietary changes
Severe abdominal pain, blood in urine or stool, or persistent vomiting
